food_name: Fish Ball Noodle Dry (Mee Pok Dry)

english: Fish Ball Noodle Dry (Mee Pok Dry)
chinese: 鱼丸粿条 — 干 (Yúwán guǒ tiáo — gān)

food_subgroup: Hawker noodle dish — flat egg noodles (mee pok) tossed in chilli sauce, dark soy, lard oil with fish balls and fish cake

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Fish Ball Noodle Dry as a prepared Singapore hawker dish. Closest parent would be a generic noodle dish with fish product toppings.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard hawker serving of fish ball noodle dry is approximately 350g, comprising ~120g cooked flat egg noodles (mee pok), ~60g fish balls (6 pieces), ~30g fish cake (2 slices), and ~140g of sauce, toppings, and broth.

nutrient_source.title: Fish Ball Noodle Dry — HPB Singapore Foodo + Component Calculation
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values derived from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Fish Ball Noodle Dry, supplemented by component-level calculation for individual ingredients (egg noodles, fish balls, fish cake, sauces, lard oil). HPB Singapore is an approved primary regional source for Singapore hawker dishes.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Egg Noodles and Mixed Noodle Dishes — University of Sydney GI Database; analogous estimate for mee pok dry
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of ~55 estimated using flat egg noodles (wheat + egg base) as the closest analogous food in the University of Sydney GI Database. Egg noodles have a published GI in the range of 46–57. The 'dry' preparation with vinegar provides a modest GI-lowering effect (acetic acid slows gastric emptying). Protein from fish balls and fish cake further moderates postprandial glucose response. GL calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 52.15) / 100 ≈ 27.5. Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content — approximately 900mg per bowl (257mg per 100g × 350g serving)
hypertension.guidance: One bowl provides approximately 900mg sodium (37–45% of the 2000–2400mg daily limit), contributed by dark soy sauce (~300mg), chilli sauce (~200mg), fish balls (~200mg), and lard seasoning (~100mg); individuals with hypertension should limit consumption to occasional servings and request reduced sauce where possible.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load — GI ~55 (low), GL ~27.5 per bowl from ~52g carbohydrate in egg noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: The GI of ~55 is in the low range and the vinegar in the sauce provides a modest GI-lowering effect; however, the glycemic load of ~27.5 per bowl is moderate-high, so individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should monitor portion size, consider sharing or leaving some noodles, and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from lard oil (~4.9g SFA per bowl) and high sodium (~900mg per bowl)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The use of lard oil as a traditional seasoning contributes approximately 4.9g saturated fat per bowl; combined with high sodium from multiple sauce components, individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and request reduced lard and sauce to lower both saturated fat and sodium intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Ball Noodle Dry (Mee Pok Dry) is one of Singapore's most iconic hawker dishes, rooted in Teochew and Hokkien Chinese culinary tradition. A standard bowl (~350g) consists of flat yellow egg noodles (mee pok, ~120g cooked), six fish balls (~60g), two slices of fish cake (~30g), and a flavourful sauce mixture of chilli sauce, dark soy sauce, lard oil, and vinegar (~140g combined with toppings). The noodles are blanched and tossed — not served in broth — giving the 'dry' designation, though a small bowl of clear fish-ball soup is typically served alongside. The texture is springy and chewy from the egg noodles, with a savoury-spicy-tangy sauce profile. Fish balls are made from processed surimi (starch-diluted, PDCAAS ~0.7). Lard oil is the traditional fat used for seasoning and is a key flavour component. Per 100g: 117 kcal, 5.7g protein, 4.0g fat (1.4g saturated), 14.9g carbohydrate, 0.4g fibre, 257mg sodium. Per bowl (~350g): ~410 kcal, ~20g protein, ~14g fat (~4.9g saturated), ~52g carbohydrate, ~900mg sodium. GI ~55 (low), GL ~27.5 (moderate-high). NOT halal — contains lard (pork fat) and possibly pork-containing fish paste. Dairy-free. Contains gluten (wheat noodles), egg, fish, and soy allergens. Source: HPB Singapore Foodo database. Confidence: HIGH.
common_user_queries: fish ball noodle GI glycemic index

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced from HPB Singapore Foodo database, which is the approved primary regional source for Singapore hawker dishes. Component-level calculation cross-checks are consistent with the per-100g values. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous food (egg noodles, GI 46–57 range) with documented estimation basis. All mandatory multilingual names present. Confidence: HIGH.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite hawker dish preparation is not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-14. water_g set to -1: moisture content for this composite dish is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo source. trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB Singapore source; lard oil does not contribute measurable trans fat in this preparation. All core macros (protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) are non-zero and well-supported. NOT halal: contains lard oil (pork fat) as a traditional seasoning ingredient and fish paste may contain pork-derived additives. Allergens: gluten (wheat egg noodles), egg, fish, soy. Portion size of 350g is the standard hawker serving reference per HPB Singapore and Project Veritas component calculation.
